project.name: Media Curator
project.summary: Media Curator is a local CLI package for organizing and deduplicating user-selected photo and video libraries with metadata extraction, perceptual hashing, LSH similarity, SQLite/LMDB state, workerpool concurrency, and WASM-assisted comparison.
goals: Own the local CLI for organizing and deduplicating large media libraries.
Own discovery, metadata extraction, perceptual hashing, LSH similarity, cache/database state, worker/WASM performance, and transfer behavior.
Keep destructive filesystem actions explicit, documented, and validated with fixtures before production release.
nonGoals: Become a hosted media library, cloud storage product, telemetry collector, or background sync service.
Own user media outside CLI-selected roots, destination directories, duplicate directories, or error directories.
Encode product-specific or customer-specific organization policies into shared core logic.

owns.name: media-curation-cli
owns.description: The user-facing CLI contract, arguments, install surface, command behavior, and local execution model.

owns.name: media-processing-pipeline
owns.description: Discovery, metadata extraction, perceptual hashing, LSH/VP-tree-style similarity, deduplication decisions, transfer behavior, cache/database state, workerpool concurrency, and WASM comparison code.

doesNotOwn: Cloud storage, hosted media libraries, background sync, or remote media retention.
User media outside explicit CLI-selected source, destination, duplicate, and error paths.
Operating-system photo library policy, external provider control planes, or product-specific organization semantics.
forbiddenCouplings: Do not upload, index, retain, or inspect user media outside explicit local CLI behavior.
Do not delete or mutate files outside user-supplied media roots, destination, duplicate, or error directories.
Do not hard-code customer-specific organization policies into shared media processing logic.
Do not publish packages from a human shell or personal token.

delivery.deployPath: No hosted deploy path is declared. Package release intent is present through Changesets and the central reusable release workflow; an older tag-driven npm publish workflow remains a release-control adoption gap.
delivery.productionProof: Required CI context, build/test evidence, fixture-backed CLI smoke proof for filesystem behavior changes, Changesets intent for package changes, and npm readback after publication.

packageRelease.releaseIntent: Changesets capture package release intent; package publishing should be bot/workflow-owned before production release claims.
packageRelease.versionPr: Version PRs should be produced by the reusable release workflow.
packageRelease.publisher: SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow with inherited repository secrets; legacy tag-driven publish remains an adoption gap.
packageRelease.publishProof: npm package readback for @sylphx/media-curator or @sylphlab/media-curator, plus build output and release notes.

guardrails: Hosted media processing, enterprise packaging, or paid roadmap changes require a decision record backed by competitor and customer analysis.
Commercial decisions must not weaken local-first privacy or filesystem safety.

gaps.description: Open CI repair PRs show the legacy validation workflow is being rehabilitated before it can be treated as stable autonomous admission.
The repository has Changesets and a central reusable release workflow, but also retains a tag-driven npm publish path; consolidate package publishing under a bot-owned release control plane.
